Full job description
Join a growing company that's making a real impact in water safety and hygiene across the UK.

Account / Business Development Manager - Water Hygiene Industry
Salary: £45,000 - £55,000
Location: Oxford

A growing and innovative company in the water hygiene industry is looking for an experienced and motivated Account / Business Development Manager.

Benefits of the Business Development Manager Opportunity:

Competitive salary (dependent on experience)
Bonus structure with uncapped earning potential
Laptop and iPhone
Gym membership
Business attire allowance
Company pension and events
Ongoing training and professional development opportunities
Duties of the Account / Business Development Manager Role:

Develop and manage a regional sales pipeline
Identify and secure new business opportunities within the water hygiene sector
Build and maintain strong relationships with new and existing clients
Prepare proposals and quotations, including L8 PPM works
Maintain accurate CRM records and manage your sales pipeline
Contribute to regional business planning and growth strategies
About the Opportunity:

Home-based role covering the South East and Midlands
Travel to meet clients, attend industry events, and conferences
One day per month at the company head office
What We're Looking For:

Experience in water hygiene, water treatment, or a related industry
Proven track record in sales, lead generation, and achieving targets
Strong communication and relationship-building skills
Ability to work independently and manage your own territory
Full UK driving licence
This Account / Business Development Manager role offers the chance to make a real impact, joining a supportive, growing team where every Account / Business Development Manager is valued and empowered to succeed.
shortCompanyReason: Growing company in the water hygiene industry making an impact across the UK.
shortRoleReason: Business development role focused on sales growth, client relationships, and regional pipeline management in water hygiene.
companySummary: A growing and innovative company in the water hygiene industry, focused on water safety and hygiene across the UK.
roleSummary: An Account / Business Development Manager role responsible for developing regional sales, winning new business, managing client relationships, and supporting growth in the water hygiene sector.
headlineAttraction: Uncapped bonus potential in a growing water hygiene business with remote working and strong benefits.
rolePurpose: To drive regional business growth by developing sales opportunities, managing client relationships, and securing new business in the water hygiene sector.
travelRequired: Travel to meet clients, attend industry events and conferences; one day per month at head office.
advertSummary: This is a permanent full-time remote Business Development Manager role in Oxford for a growing water hygiene company. The role covers the South East and Midlands, with travel to clients and events, and one day per month at head office. It offers a £45,000 - £55,000 salary plus bonus and benefits.
personalisedIntro: I’m looking at this as a role where commercial follow-through matters as much as winning the work in the first place. My background is in bringing structure to fast-moving environments, improving performance, and keeping delivery and reporting tight enough to support growth.
whyThisRole: This looks like a role for someone who can own a territory, build a pipeline, and keep relationships moving without needing close day-to-day supervision. That combination of independent working, client contact, proposal work and CRM discipline fits the way I like to operate.
companyPridePoints: Real impact in water safety and hygiene
Bonus structure with uncapped earning potential
coreResponsibilities: Develop and manage a regional sales pipeline
Identify and secure new business opportunities
Include L8 PPM works in quotations
Contribute to regional business planning and growth strategies
essentialRequirements: Experience in water hygiene, water treatment, or a related industry
Proven track record in sales and lead generation
Ability to manage your own territory
preferredRequirements: Experience attending industry events and conferences
impliedStrategicGoals: Market expansion in the water hygiene sector
possibleHeadlineFacts: Coverage of South East and Midlands
Bonus structure with uncapped earning potential

personalisedContent.heroPositioning: Commercially minded operator who builds pipeline, control and client trust in complex settings.
personalisedContent.personalisedOpening: I’m looking at this as a role where commercial follow-through matters as much as winning the work in the first place. My background is in bringing structure to fast-moving environments, improving performance, and keeping delivery and reporting tight enough to support growth.
personalisedContent.whyThisCompany: Penguin Recruitment is offering a mix I value: a growing business, a clear bonus opportunity, remote working, and a team that says it wants people to feel valued and empowered. The emphasis on training and professional development also stands out to me, because I’ve always worked best in places that expect strong results but still invest in people.
personalisedContent.whyThisRole: This looks like a role for someone who can own a territory, build a pipeline, and keep relationships moving without needing close day-to-day supervision. That combination of independent working, client contact, proposal work and CRM discipline fits the way I like to operate.

selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve worked in a role where tighter control, better reporting and stronger cash performance mattered, and I helped deliver real improvement. That gives me a practical base for a sales role where numbers, follow-through and accurate records matter.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a credibility anchor for commercial discipline and results focus.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve worked to improve financial performance, not just report on it.

selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve worked in a business that was growing and needed more disciplined delivery, better resilience and stronger execution. That’s relevant here because this role needs someone who can manage activity, keep momentum and support growth in a practical way.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as supporting evidence for growth, pace and self-management.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’m used to working in a business that needs pace, control and growth at the same time.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Complex annual projects and team leadership
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve managed complex workloads, supported a team and kept service standards moving in a regulated environment. That translates well to handling multiple clients, priorities and follow-ups across a territory.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a supporting example for coordination, communication and reliability.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’m comfortable keeping multiple priorities moving without losing control.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: My reading of this role is that it needs someone who can create new business, keep existing accounts warm, and stay on top of the detail behind the sales process. It also needs someone who can work independently across a territory, handle proposals and CRM properly, and build trust with clients over time.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Proven track record in sales and lead generation
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked in a scale-up environment where stronger execution and better operational discipline supported more orders won and improved margin.
experienceMappings.relevance: It shows I understand growth pressure and how to support it with consistent follow-through.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Ability to manage your own territory
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve handled complex, competing priorities and kept work moving across a demanding environment without needing constant oversight.
experienceMappings.relevance: That matters in a remote territory role where I’d need to plan my own time and keep momentum.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked in roles where communication, coordination and service standards had to stay strong while supporting a team and external stakeholders.
experienceMappings.relevance: This role depends on keeping clients engaged and building repeat business, not just opening doors.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Commercial control and accurate CRM-style discipline
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve built reporting and financial control into my work, including tighter oversight of performance and cash.
experienceMappings.relevance: That gives me a good base for keeping pipeline information accurate and useful.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve led day-to-day operations in a growing business where I had to stay on top of delivery, people and performance.
experienceMappings.relevance: A territory-based role needs someone who can organise themselves and keep standards high without close supervision.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I haven’t listed direct proposal-writing examples in my CV, but I do have a strong background in structured reporting, operational control and working with detail.
experienceMappings.relevance: That suggests I’d be comfortable learning the commercial format quickly and handling the detail carefully.

focusAreasToBring.title: Pipeline discipline
focusAreasToBring.summary: I’d keep the pipeline clear, current and usable, so activity, next steps and opportunities are easy to track and act on.

focusAreasToBring.title: Client follow-through
focusAreasToBring.summary: I’d focus on keeping conversations warm, responding properly, and building enough trust that clients see me as reliable rather than transactional.

focusAreasToBring.title: Commercial accuracy
focusAreasToBring.summary: I’d treat proposals, quotations and CRM records as part of the job, not admin after the fact, because accuracy affects conversion and confidence.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: I bring a background in structured, performance-led roles where I’ve had to balance growth, control and stakeholder management. I’m not coming from water hygiene directly, but I do bring the kind of discipline, independence and commercial awareness that this role seems to need.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I’d expect to help build a more visible and consistent regional pipeline, keep client relationships active, and support better conversion from opportunity to quotation. I’d also expect to bring steady control to the process around CRM, follow-up and territory management, so the sales effort is easier to manage and scale.
companyHighlights: Growing company in the water hygiene industry
Remote working with travel to clients and events
Supportive team that values and empowers people
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work well in environments that expect ownership, clear standards and practical follow-through. The mix of support, growth and accountability in Penguin Recruitment feels aligned with how I like to work.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I’d get clear on the regional patch, the client base, the sales process, and what good looks like in the CRM and quotation workflow.
I’d focus on structured follow-up, active pipeline management and getting comfortable with the kinds of opportunities and client conversations that matter most.
I’d look to improve consistency in prospecting, proposal quality and account contact, while feeding back useful territory insight into growth planning.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I’d bring a steady, commercially minded approach to growing the region and keeping the sales process under control.
closingProofPoints: I’ve improved financial performance and working-capital control in a regulated environment.
I’ve supported growth and stronger execution in a scale-up business.
I’ve built reporting and tracking systems where accuracy and follow-through mattered.
contentNotes: I have not claimed direct water hygiene experience because it is not shown in the CV or evidence bank.
I have not invented proposal-writing examples; I’ve framed that as a transferable strength from reporting and control work.
The evidence bank is stronger on operations and performance than pure sales, so I’ve positioned the fit around commercial discipline, territory ownership and follow-through.
